verses.text: Do not your alms 'to be seen of men'; give in secret, and 'thy Father... shall reward thee openly.' Hidden giving. God rewards charity done for Him, not for show.
Do not your alms 'to be seen of men'; give in secret, and 'thy Father... shall reward thee openly.' Hidden giving. God rewards charity done for Him, not for show.
Do not your alms 'to be seen of men'; give in secret, and 'thy Father... shall reward thee openly.' Hidden giving. God rewards charity done for Him, not for show.
Do not your alms 'to be seen of men'; give in secret, and 'thy Father... shall reward thee openly.' Hidden giving. God rewards charity done for Him, not for show.
Pray not as the hypocrites; enter thy closet, and 'pray to thy Father which is in secret.' Private prayer. The Father hears the heart that seeks Him alone.
Pray not as the hypocrites; enter thy closet, and 'pray to thy Father which is in secret.' Private prayer. The Father hears the heart that seeks Him alone.
Use not 'vain repetitions' as the heathen; your Father knows your need before you ask. Simple prayer. God seeks the sincere word, not the many words.
Use not 'vain repetitions' as the heathen; your Father knows your need before you ask. Simple prayer. God seeks the sincere word, not the many words.

'After this manner... pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name... deliver us from evil.' The model prayer. Christ teaches us to hallow God, seek His will, and ask our daily bread.

'After this manner... pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name... deliver us from evil.' The model prayer. Christ teaches us to hallow God, seek His will, and ask our daily bread.

'After this manner... pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name... deliver us from evil.' The model prayer. Christ teaches us to hallow God, seek His will, and ask our daily bread.

'After this manner... pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name... deliver us from evil.' The model prayer. Christ teaches us to hallow God, seek His will, and ask our daily bread.

'After this manner... pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name... deliver us from evil.' The model prayer. Christ teaches us to hallow God, seek His will, and ask our daily bread.
If ye forgive men, your Father forgives you; if not, neither will He. Forgiveness mutual. The unforgiving heart shuts itself out from pardon.
If ye forgive men, your Father forgives you; if not, neither will He. Forgiveness mutual. The unforgiving heart shuts itself out from pardon.
When ye fast, look not sad to be seen; 'appear not unto men to fast, but unto thy Father.' Secret devotion. Fasting is for God's eye, not man's praise.
When ye fast, look not sad to be seen; 'appear not unto men to fast, but unto thy Father.' Secret devotion. Fasting is for God's eye, not man's praise.
When ye fast, look not sad to be seen; 'appear not unto men to fast, but unto thy Father.' Secret devotion. Fasting is for God's eye, not man's praise.
Lay up treasure in heaven, not on earth; 'for where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.' Heavenly riches. What we treasure shapes the bent of our hearts.
Lay up treasure in heaven, not on earth; 'for where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.' Heavenly riches. What we treasure shapes the bent of our hearts.
Lay up treasure in heaven, not on earth; 'for where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.' Heavenly riches. What we treasure shapes the bent of our hearts.
The eye is the body's light; if it be single, the whole body is full of light. Singleness of aim. A heart fixed on God fills the life with light.
The eye is the body's light; if it be single, the whole body is full of light. Singleness of aim. A heart fixed on God fills the life with light.
'No man can serve two masters... ye cannot serve God and mammon.' A clear choice. The heart cannot be divided between God and wealth.
Take no anxious thought for life; the fowls neither sow nor reap, yet your Father feeds them; 'are ye not much better?' Trustful ease. God's care for the birds assures us of His care for us.
Take no anxious thought for life; the fowls neither sow nor reap, yet your Father feeds them; 'are ye not much better?' Trustful ease. God's care for the birds assures us of His care for us.
Take no anxious thought for life; the fowls neither sow nor reap, yet your Father feeds them; 'are ye not much better?' Trustful ease. God's care for the birds assures us of His care for us.
Consider the lilies; if God so clothe the grass, much more will He clothe you, 'O ye of little faith.' Provision promised. Worry betrays a small view of God's goodness.
Consider the lilies; if God so clothe the grass, much more will He clothe you, 'O ye of little faith.' Provision promised. Worry betrays a small view of God's goodness.
Consider the lilies; if God so clothe the grass, much more will He clothe you, 'O ye of little faith.' Provision promised. Worry betrays a small view of God's goodness.

'Seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you'; take no thought for tomorrow. First things first. Put God before all, and trust Him with the rest.

'Seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you'; take no thought for tomorrow. First things first. Put God before all, and trust Him with the rest.
